articles.headline: 2026 World Cup: Which 48 teams qualified for the tournament?
articles.description: The race to qualify for the 2026 FIFA World Cup is complete. Here's how it all went down across the globe.

images.name: Sweden celebrate [1296x729]
images.credit: Linnea Rheborg - UEFA/UEFA via Getty Images

images.name: Scotland celebrate [1296x729]
images.credit: Alan Harvey/SNS Group via Getty Images

images.name: World Cup trophy New York [1296x729]
images.credit: Mike Stobe - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

images.name: Marcotti: Italy have 'no excuses' for another World Cup failure
images.caption: Gab Marcotti reacts to Italy failing to qualify for a third straight World Cup after losing to Bosnia and Herzegovina on penalties.

articles.headline: Could Morocco beat Brazil in Group C at the World Cup?
articles.description: Sam Ogunleye and Ed Dove discuss the strength of Morocco's squad as well as the potential to defeat group favourites Brazil.

images.name: Could Morocco beat Brazil in Group C at the World Cup?
images.caption: Sam Ogunleye and Ed Dove discuss the strength of Morocco's squad as well as the potential to defeat group favourites Brazil.

articles.headline: New Adidas World Cup jerseys rated: Argentina, Mexico, Spain
articles.description: Adidas has released a huge batch of home kits for 22 countries, many of whom have either qualified or still hope to qualify for the FIFA 2026 World Cup.

articles.headline: Messi: It's a joy to continue at Inter Miami
articles.description: Lionel Messi says he's "very happy" to continue at Inter Miami after signing a new deal with the club.

images.name: Messi: It's a joy to continue at Inter Miami
images.caption: Lionel Messi says he's "very happy" to continue at Inter Miami after signing a new deal with the club.

articles.headline: Carlo Ancelotti speaks about life as Brazil's head coach
articles.description: Carlo Ancelotti is "having a great time" living in Brazil and managing the national team ahead of the 2026 World Cup.

images.name: Carlo Ancelotti speaks about life as Brazil's head coach
images.caption: Carlo Ancelotti is "having a great time" living in Brazil and managing the national team ahead of the 2026 World Cup.

articles.headline: Raphinha admits he was tempted by a move to Saudi Arabia
articles.description: Raphinha speaks about a transfer offer from Saudi Arabia before Hansi Flick took over Barcelona in 2024.

images.name: Raphinha admits he was tempted by a move to Saudi Arabia
images.caption: Raphinha speaks about a transfer offer from Saudi Arabia before Hansi Flick took over Barcelona in 2024.
